title: Cast interview vids on End of Eva website
display_title: Cast interview vids on End of Eva website
wikitext:
[]: Cast interview vids on End of Eva website             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Nov 03, 2011 2:58 pm Just in case folks dont know about the end site thats still there: http://endofeva.com/ Interviews with Spike and others still there for download

[]: Re: Cast interview vids on End of Eva website             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 03, 2011 3:10 pm Jayfive wrote: Just in case folks dont know about the end site thats still there: http://endofeva.com/ Interviews with Spike and others still there for download

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Nov 03, 2011 3:39 pm Ah hell. Well at least folks know the Eoe/D and R microsites are still there :)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 03, 2011 4:14 pm Jayfive wrote: Ah hell. Well at least folks know the Eoe/D and R microsites are still there :)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 03, 2011 4:31 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't understand why they're still online, Manga entertainment hasn't had the rights for five years now. Who's still paying for these sites?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Nov 03, 2011 5:00 pm Somebody archive this shit fast It's historical

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Nov 03, 2011 6:54 pm Domain Name: ENDOFEVA.COM Created on: 27-Nov-01 Expires on: 27-Nov-11

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Nov 04, 2011 4:06 am It just so happens I have a spare copy of EoE that I was putting on ebay and I thought I'd see if that URL listed on the back of the box still worked. I can assure you that there was absolutely no muffled squeak of alarm when I was greeted with the creepy smiling GNR half-head. If youre going to archive it, do the D&R site too: www.deathandrebirth.com

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 04, 2011 7:19 am Jayfive wrote: If youre going to archive it, do the D&R site too: www.deathandrebirth.com

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Fri Nov 04, 2011 10:33 am I feel like it's the end of an era.:(

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 04, 2011 11:49 am bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: I feel like it's the end of an era.:(

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Nov 04, 2011 12:25 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: I feel like it's the end of an era.:(

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Nov 04, 2011 5:05 pm Every second an old era ends and a new era begins.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Nov 04, 2011 5:34 pm Trajan wrote: Every second an old era ends and a new era begins.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Nov 04, 2011 6:34 pm I don't find that depressing. If anything, it symbolizes change. Change is good... usually.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Nov 05, 2011 6:44 am Stryker wrote: I don't find that depressing. If anything, it symbolizes change. Change is good... usually.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Nov 05, 2011 1:19 pm Domain Name: ENDOFEVA.COM Created on: 27-Nov-01 Expires on: 27-Nov-11

[]:             Quote Post by sbtekno » Sat Nov 05, 2011 9:54 pm Went to the site. Saw the half-head. NOT GOING BACK.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sun Nov 06, 2011 12:13 am We ought to see if the website can provide us with any clues about who made the Eva movie DVD menus, while we still can. WE NEED TO FIND OUT WHO DID THOSE.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Nov 06, 2011 3:18 am gatotsu911 wrote: We ought to see if the website can provide us with any clues about who made the Eva movie DVD menus, while we still can. WE NEED TO FIND OUT WHO DID THOSE.

[]: Re: Cast interview vids on End of Eva website             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 03, 2011 3:10 pm Jayfive wrote: J
